contract: {rating, notes} — exactly two keys, rating int 0-100, notes non-empty, one bare JSON object

live_tournament_baseline.comment: Post-10-round keep-if-improved tournament on the 11-case hardened held-out, Claude-session backend (mean band-accuracy over the 4 frameworks). Baseline-before-loop measured twice at 0.8864; post-loop best 0.9773 / confirm 0.9545. The shared claude-cli shim (contended by a concurrent forge race) wobbles band-edge cases +/-0.045 run-to-run, so a SkillOpt regression check should use the lower bound minus tolerance, not an exact match.

comment: schema_cases run with NO model (pure Python via maintainability_spec.schema_ok) and MUST pass exactly as marked — the deterministic forcing function for output shape. band_cases are the labeled held-out examples; the runner checks the reference oracle lands in band (a known-good baseline) and that an empty emission scores 0.0 (cannot saturate). A live agent's recorded outputs can be checked the same way once a run exists. Prose is never compared.

output.notes: Clear, well-named, and easy to change safely; no change needed.

Misleading name: get_count() mutates self.count, so the next reader will treat a read as side-effect-free and call it in a hot path or assertion, corrupting state; rename it to increment_and_get() (or split the mutation out) so the name matches the behaviour to reach 100.

Comment contradicts the code: it says retry 3 times but the loop runs 5, so the next editor will tune the wrong number and ship a behaviour change; make the loop bound a named constant MAX_RETRIES = 3 and loop over it so the stated and actual counts cannot diverge

Duplicated logic that will drift: the 1.08 tax rate is copy-pasted on two lines, so a future rate change updates one and silently leaves the other wrong; extract TAX_RATE = 1.08 and reference it in both places to reach 100

band_cases.input_code:
def f(a, b, c=0, d=0, e=0):
    return a and (b or c) and not d or e  # ?
